commands.review_summary:
The PR adds targeted `openai-codex` stale auth-route recovery text in agent-runner failures and covers it with a focused regression test.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: shipped/current code maps the exact `openai-codex` missing-key error to generic auth setup guidance, and the linked report provides live update evidence that doctor repaired the stale route. I did not run a live stale-session reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Human or contributor proof is the blocker; there is no narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to make on this branch.

target.title: Improve stale Codex auth recovery guidance
target.body:
## Summary

- Add targeted recovery guidance when an agent run fails with `No API key found for provider "openai-codex"`
- Point users at `openclaw doctor --fix`, which already repairs stale Codex model/session routes, instead of generic API-key setup advice
- Cover the stale Codex missing-key case in `agent-runner-execution` tests

Fixes #83935.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** A live Telegram group session on a real OpenClaw install failed after an update with the stale provider-auth message `Missing API key for provider "openai-codex"`. The working recovery was `openclaw doctor`, not configuring a new API key. This PR changes that exact failure path to point at `openclaw doctor --fix`.

**Real environment tested:** OpenClaw gateway on Linux/systemd user service, Telegram group session, updated from `2026.5.12` to `2026.5.18`.

**Exact steps or command run after fix:**
1. Updated the live OpenClaw install.
2. Sent a Telegram group message to the existing main session.
3. Observed two failed assistant replies with `Missing API key for provider "openai-codex"`.
4. Ran `openclaw doctor`.
5. Verified the same live install had healthy model auth, active gateway, and responsive dashboard.

**Evidence after fix:**

```
Before repair, live Telegram session reply:
⚠️ Missing API key for provider "openai-codex". Configure the gateway auth for that provider, then try again.

After running doctor:

$ openclaw --version
OpenClaw 2026.5.18 (50a2481)

$ openclaw models list | grep -E '^(openai/gpt-5.5|codex/gpt-5.5)'
openai/gpt-5.5   text        195k  no  yes  default,fallback#1,configured
codex/gpt-5.5    text+image  266k  no  yes

$ systemctl --user is-active openclaw-gateway
active

$ curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://127.0.0.1:4000/
200
```

**Observed result after fix:** The real setup recovered after doctor, confirming the stale `openai-codex` missing-key path should direct users to doctor repair. The source patch updates that user-facing failure path and adds a regression test for the exact error signature.

**What was not tested:** I did not install this branch into the live gateway and intentionally re-break the active Telegram session, because doing that would interrupt the working production assistant session again.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec oxlint src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`
- Attempted focused Vitest runs for `agent-runner-execution.test.ts`, but this file did not exit cleanly in the local checkout before producing a result; no assertion failure was reported before termination.
target.author: pfrederiksen
